contaminant.name: Insect Impact Residue
contaminant.description: Insect-residue contamination, this protein-rich deposit from insect bodies, forms when flying insects collide and smear across surfaces during motion, then dry under air and heat exposure, thus creating hardened adhesive layers over time. Unique characteristics include irregular splatters with yellowish hues and fibrous textures, distinguishing from dirt or pollen by sticky organic composition and biological resilience, while on materials like float glass or stainless steel, it adheres tightly, etches lightly under sunlight, dulls surfaces, and traps particles in pores, so still persists after exposure. Key challenges arise from bonding strength that resists solvents and scraping, mechanical methods spread it further, but laser cleaning proves effective as pulses vaporize organics precisely without abrasion, thus targets residue selectively and already restores surface uniformity with no secondary damage.

fireExplosionRisk.description: Minimal fire risk with standard precautions and adequate ventilation
fireExplosionRisk.mitigation: Standard fire safety precautions, extinguisher available within 15m

toxicGasRisk.description: Multiple toxic compounds detected: Carbon Monoxide, Carbon Dioxide, Hydrogen Cyanide - requires enhanced protection
toxicGasRisk.mitigation: Half-face or full-face respirator with organic vapor/particulate cartridges, adequate ventilation. WARNING: Polycyclic Aromatic Hydrocarbons (PAHs) - known carcinogen(s), minimize exposure

visibilityHazard.description: Moderate visibility reduction (40-60%), significant particulate haze
visibilityHazard.mitigation: Ensure clear sight lines, use source extraction, maintain awareness of surroundings

ventilationRequirements.rationale: Standard industrial ventilation (12 ACH) for particulate control
